title: Variability of the bottom-simulating reflector (BSR) and its association with tectonic structures in the Chilean margin between Arauco Gulf (37°S) and Valdivia (40°S)
abstract: Multichannel seismic reflection data recorded between Arauco Gulf (37°S) and Valdivia (40°S) were analyzed to characterize the variability of the BSR. The BSR is discontinuous and interrupted by submarine valleys, canyons, faults or fractures, with occurrence more common south of Mocha Island. Tectonic uplift and structural instability create missing or double BSRs in some areas, and higher BSR amplitudes are associated with thrust faults in the accretionary prism.

location.description: Chilean margin between Arauco Gulf (37°S) and Valdivia (40°S); BSR variability study on multiple seismic profiles

seismic_lines.name: SPOC and legacy MCS profiles — Arauco to Valdivia (37°–40°S)

acquisition.vessel: R/V SONNE (SO161 SPOC, 2001) and legacy Conrad/GEOMAR cruises

data.name: SPOC and Legacy MCS Data — Arauco–Valdivia 37°–40°S
data.description: Multiple MCS reflection lines from SPOC (SO161) and legacy cruises analyzed for BSR variability between 37°–40°S; BSR is discontinuous and influenced by thrust faults in the accretionary prism. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — Arauco–Valdivia Region
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from SO161 and other cruises used to correlate BSR distribution with seafloor morphology. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

_data_note: Uses SPOC (SO161) and legacy MCS profiles. MGDS accessible data.

processing.summary: Multiple MCS reflection profiles from the Chilean margin between 37°S and 40°S (SPOC SO161 and legacy cruises) were analyzed specifically for BSR characteristics. The BSR variability — including discontinuous segments, double BSRs, and amplitude anomalies — is correlated with tectonic structures including submarine valleys, thrust faults, and uplift zones. Velocity analysis is applied for gas hydrate estimation.
processing.notes: BSR-focused study using existing SPOC and legacy seismic profiles; no new acquisition

analysis_notes: Marine Geophysical Research paper; behind paywall. Uses SPOC SO161 data accessible via MGDS.
